evals.prompt: Set up self-improving agents in my Node.js project that uses Express, Jest for testing, and ESLint
evals.expected_output: Requires Entire CLI. Creates .claude/agents/ with 6 agent files (including session-analyzer), gaps.md, progress.md. Configures Entire hooks. Agent files should mention Jest and ESLint by name. CLAUDE.md should have the harness section appended.

evals.prompt: I want agents that can build and improve code in my Python Django project. We use pytest and have Playwright for e2e tests.
evals.expected_output: Requires Entire CLI. Creates .claude/agents/ with 6 agent files (including session-analyzer), gaps.md, progress.md. Configures Entire hooks. Builder should reference pytest, e2e-tester should reference Playwright. Should detect Django as framework.

evals.prompt: scaffold agent harness into this empty repo — no framework, no tests, just a README
evals.expected_output: Requires Entire CLI. Creates .claude/agents/ with session-analyzer plus standard agents, gaps.md, progress.md. Configures Entire hooks. Should handle the 'no test runner' and 'no e2e' cases gracefully without broken placeholders.

evals.prompt: Set up self-improving agents in my project. I have the entire CLI installed at ~/.local/bin/entire and want telemetry enabled.
evals.expected_output: Detects Entire CLI, asks user before configuring. Creates session-analyzer.md in .claude/agents/. Runs entire configure. Adds deny permission for .entire/metadata. CLAUDE.md mentions session analysis. Orchestrator EVOLVE phase references session-analyzer.
